without_skill_failures: Output uses v4 strongly-typed ClientCredentialsClientName.Parse() for the client name (not a raw string)
Output wires up RevokeRefreshTokenAsync() in the cookie OnSigningOut event handler
Output replaces IClientCredentialsTokenManagementService with IClientCredentialsTokenManager
Output uses ClientCredentialsClientName.Parse() for the client name argument instead of a raw string
Output checks tokenResult.Succeeded before calling .GetToken() instead of directly accessing .Value
Output removes or notes that AddDistributedMemoryCache() is no longer required because v4 uses HybridCache automatically
Output uses strongly-typed property setters like ClientId.Parse(), ClientSecret.Parse(), and Scope.Parse()
Output loads the DPoP JWK from configuration or secure storage rather than generating a new RSA key at startup
Output warns against or avoids generating ephemeral keys with RSA.Create() on every process restart
Output registers a client credentials HTTP client for the master data API using AddClientAccessTokenHttpClient or AddHttpClient with AddClientAccessTokenHandler
Output calls AddOpenIdConnectAccessTokenManagement() to enable both user and client token management
Output implements IHybridCacheSerializer<ClientCredentialsToken> using IDataProtector for encryption
Output registers the custom serializer via AddHybridCache().AddSerializer<ClientCredentialsToken, ...>()
Output identifies that .GetToken() is called without first checking .Succeeded, which will throw InvalidOperationException on failure
Output identifies that AddClientCredentialsTokenHandler is used without AddDefaultAccessTokenResiliency(), meaning 401 responses won't be retried
Output chains .AddUserAccessTokenHandler() on the gRPC client registration to attach user tokens automatically
Output includes or references AddOpenIdConnectAccessTokenManagement() as prerequisite for user token management
Calls AddBlazorServerAccessTokenManagement<T>() with a custom IUserTokenStore implementation
The custom IUserTokenStore implements GetTokenAsync, StoreTokenAsync, and ClearTokenAsync methods
Shows capturing tokens during the OnTokenValidated OIDC event to persist them to the custom store
Sets the JWT Audience to the authorization server's issuer URL, NOT the token endpoint URL
Warns about CVE-2025-27370 or CVE-2025-27371 related to incorrect audience values
Implements ITokenRequestCustomizer interface with a Customize method
The Customize method accepts HttpRequestMessage, TokenRequestParameters, and CancellationToken
Uses the 'with' expression or equivalent to create modified TokenRequestParameters (e.g., setting Resource or Scope per tenant)
Passes the customizer instance to AddClientCredentialsTokenHandler or AddUserAccessTokenHandler
